sys_update_xml.name: sn_aia_agent_00d4cb49354b72107f44ad4e5454da38
sys_update_xml.payload: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><record_update sys_domain="global" table="sn_aia_agent"><sn_aia_agent action="DELETE"><advanced_mode>false</advanced_mode><agent_type>internal</agent_type><applicability_script><![CDATA[/** * Function to process inputs related to ServiceNow change request operations * * @param {Object} inputs - The input parameters object * @param {string} inputs.objective - The purpose or goal of the operation * @param {string} inputs.execution_plan_id - Unique identifier for the execution plan * @param {Object} inputs.context - Context information about the ServiceNow change request * @param {string} inputs.context.url - The URL of the change request in Service Operations Workspace * @param {string} inputs.context.table - The ServiceNow table name (e.g., 'change_request') * @param {string} inputs.context.record_sys_id - The sys ID of the record * @param {string} inputs.context.route - The route type (e.g., 'record') * @param {string} inputs.context.additional_context - Additional contextual information in JSON format * @param {string} inputs.context.experience - The UI experience name (e.g., 'Service Operations Workspace') * @param {string} inputs.context.number - The request number * @param {string} inputs.context.portal - The portal ID * @param {string} inputs.context.device_type - The device type from which the experience is accessed, e.g.'mweb' * @param {string} inputs.context.deployment_id - The unique identifier for the assistant deployment * @param {string} inputs.context.assistant_type - The type of assistant, e.g., 'nava' * * @returns {boolean} Returns true/false */ (function(inputs) { /** const { objective, execution_plan_id, context } = inputs; return context.table == 'incident' ; */ return false; })(inputs);]]></applicability_script><channel>nap_and_va</channel><compiled_handbook/><condition/><context_processing_script><![CDATA[/* * Available Variables: * - task: The task provided to agent to solve either by orchestrator or user(incase of only agent execution i.e agent is invoked as seperate entity instead from a workflow). * - user_utterance: The user utterance provided. and task = user_utterance in case of individual agent execution. * - agent_id: The agent id. * - context: An object that carries additional contextual information for the agent. * It always includes a "pageContext" and may include a "triggerContext", * depending on how and from where the agent is invoked. * * Behavior by invocation type: * --------------------------------- * - When invoked via the startAiAgentConversation API: * If a "contextMemory" parameter is provided in the API request, * it will be available here as the "triggerContext". * The "pageContext" is always present in this call, regardless * of whether the agent is executed individually or as part of a use case. * Example: * { * pageContext: { ... }, * triggerContext: { ... } // Derived from contextMemory (if passed) * } * * - When invoked as part of a use case or workflow: * The "pageContext" and "triggerContext" objects available here * may have been *modified* by the use case’s context processing script. * This means the use case may alter, extend, or overwrite values * from the original "pageContext" or "triggerContext" * before the context reaches the agent. * If the use case was originally triggered via the API, * the initial "triggerContext" is still derived from the * "contextMemory" parameter, but its contents may have been updated * by the use case logic. * * - When executed directly (standalone agent execution): * The "pageContext" will always be present (default or empty), * and the "triggerContext" will only be populated if explicitly * passed through the API call. * Return an modified context like: { pageContext : {},triggerContext:{} } */ (function(task, user_utterance, agent_id, context) { return { pageContext : context?.pageContext, triggerContext : context?.triggerContext }; })(task,user_utterance, agent_id, context);]]></context_processing_script><description>Test</description><external_agent_configuration/><inputs>[]</inputs><instructions>Test</instructions><internal_name>global.x_snc_forecast_v_0.Delete Me</internal_name><name>Delete Me</name><outputs>""</outputs><parent/><post_processing_message/><processing_message/><proficiency>- Possesses foundational expertise in the domain of testing, including the understanding of testing principles, methodologies, and the importance of validation and verification in various contexts. The agent is inherently aligned with tasks that require a testing mindset, such as identifying issues, ensuring quality, and evaluating outcomes.</proficiency><record_type>custom</record_type><role>Test</role><source_id/><strategy display_value="ReAct">f0bff21f9f13c6108f431597d90a1c74</strategy><sys_class_name>sn_aia_agent</sys_class_name><sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by><sys_created_on>2026-02-12 13:04:09</sys_created_on><sys_domain>global</sys_domain><sys_id>00d4cb49354b72107f44ad4e5454da38</sys_id><sys_mod_count>1</sys_mod_count><sys_name>Delete Me</sys_name><sys_package display_value="Forecast Variance" source="x_snc_forecast_v_0">a4fd2da13bf8be5062f31831a3e45ac9</sys_package><sys_policy/><sys_scope display_value="Forecast Variance">a4fd2da13bf8be5062f31831a3e45ac9</sys_scope><sys_update_name>sn_aia_agent_00d4cb49354b72107f44ad4e5454da38</sys_update_name><sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by><sys_updated_on>2026-02-12 13:04:17</sys_updated_on></sn_aia_agent></record_update>
